chief_complaint: A 70-year-old woman presented with left-sided mastitis unresponsive to treatment and persistent hypoxia.
history: The patient had chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) and was taking cetirizine and indapamide. She had a history of mastitis and was experiencing persistent hypoxia despite clear chest imaging.
exam: Vital signs: peripheral oxygen saturation 86%. Physical examination revealed left-sided mastitis.
labs: Arterial blood gas analysis: elevated MetHb level of 26.5%. Genetic testing: homozygous pathogenic variant in cytochrome b5 reductase.
